description_long: This object resembles a large, fossilized skull with prominent, downward-curving saber-like fangs at the front, reminiscent of a prehistoric predator. The surface is rough and matte, with a dark gray coloration and lighter beige highlights around the teeth and jawline. The eye sockets are deep and hollow, giving the object a menacing appearance, while the nasal cavity is broad and open. The overall form is robust and compact, with a flattened cranium and pronounced cheekbones. The lower jaw is not present, and the structure appears solid and heavy, with no visible segmentation or appendages. The object evokes the impression of a formidable ancient creature, possibly a saber-toothed cat, rather than a typical crustacean.
description: A dark, fossil-like skull with large saber-shaped fangs and deep eye sockets, featuring a rough, matte surface and robust structure.
description_view_0: The left side of the skull is visible, highlighting the curvature of the fangs and the depth of the eye socket.
description_view_1: The front view emphasizes the prominent saber-like fangs and the wide nasal opening.
description_view_2: The right side of the skull is shown, mirroring the left with visible fangs and cheekbone structure.
description_view_3: The rear left angle reveals the back of the cranium and the hollow interior of the eye socket.
